primaryStudies.citation: Jastreboff AM, Kaplan LM, Frias JP, et al. Triple-Hormone-Receptor Agonist Retatrutide for Obesity - A Phase 2 Trial. N Engl J Med. 2023;389(6):514-526.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: 24.2% mean weight loss at 48 weeks with 12mg dose; weight loss trajectory not yet plateaued; improvements in cardiometabolic measures

primaryStudies.citation: Rosenstock J, Frias J, Jastreboff AM, et al. Retatrutide, a GIP, GLP-1 and glucagon receptor agonist, for people with type 2 diabetes. Lancet. 2023;402(10401):529-544.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: 16.9% weight loss at 36 weeks; HbA1c reduced by 2.02%; 82% reached HbA1c target of 6.5%

primaryStudies.citation: Sanyal AJ, Kaplan LM, Frias JP, et al. Triple hormone receptor agonist retatrutide for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease: a randomized phase 2a trial. Nat Med. 2024;30(7):2037-2048.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Liver fat reduction of 81-82% with higher doses; 89-93% achieved normal liver fat levels

primaryStudies.citation: Urva S, Coskun T, Loh MT, et al. LY3437943, a novel triple GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptor agonist in people with type 2 diabetes: a phase 1b, multicentre trial. Lancet. 2022;400(10366):1869-1881.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: First-in-human: dose-dependent glucose lowering and weight loss; half-life ~6 days enabling weekly dosing

primaryStudies.citation: Coskun T, Wu Q, Schloot NC, et al. Effects of retatrutide on body composition in people with type 2 diabetes: a substudy of a phase 2 trial. Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol. 2025;13(8):674-684.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Detailed body composition analysis; fat mass vs lean mass changes characterized

primaryStudies.citation: Giblin K, Kaplan LM, Somers VK, et al. Retatrutide for the treatment of obesity, obstructive sleep apnea and knee osteoarthritis: Rationale and design of the TRIUMPH registrational clinical trials. Diabetes Obes Metab. 2026;28(1):83-93.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive Phase 3 program design; multiple indications including OSA and knee OA

systematicReviews.citation: Abdrabou Abouelmagd A, et al. Efficacy and safety of retatrutide for obesity: a systematic review and meta-analysis. Proc Bayl Univ Med Cent. 2025;38(3):291-303.
systematicReviews.keyFindings: Dose-dependent weight loss; consistent efficacy across 4mg, 8mg, 12mg doses

evidenceSummary.keyResult: Up to 28.7% weight loss in Phase 3 (TRIUMPH-4); first triple agonist with positive Phase 3 data
evidenceSummary.globalCoverage: 124 publications in PubMed from 30+ countries; 9 registered clinical trials across 25+ countries
